channel.title: Eternal Record
channel.description: Daily cinematic mythology stories — 90-second vertical videos.
channel.itunes:author: Ayush Rana

item.title: The Envious Eyes of Sagar
item.description: Today's story will change how you see everything... In the kingdom of Chedi, there lived a powerful ruler named Sagar, whose armies were feared by all. His kingdom was prosperous, and his people loved him. But Sagar was consumed by a growing sense of Envy. He felt that his neighbor, the kingdom of Haihaya, was more prosperous and stronger. He became obsessed with their wealth and power, spending every waking moment thinking of ways to surpass them. He built grander temples, more impressive palaces, and a massive army to rival Haihaya's. But no matter how hard he tried, he couldn't shake the feeling that he was inferior. His advisors and friends warned him that his Envy was eating away at his soul, but Sagar refused to listen. He was convinced that with enough wealth and power, he would finally be the greatest king of all. One day, while on a hunting expedition, Sagar caught a glimpse of his son, the future king, playing with children in the palace courtyard. For a moment, he forgot about his Envy and smiled. But that moment was short-lived. His Envy crept back in, and he became consumed by a desire to surpass even his own son. This is Envy — the constant comparison to others. Today, social media feeds us endless comparisons, making us feel like we're never good enough. Ask yourself, what are you comparing yourself to, and why?
